input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and .NET MAUI has been moving toward using `WindowInsets` and edge-to-edge display. In .NET 10, the behavior changed.

Let me provide a concise answer based on my knowledge:

In .NET 10 (and with newer Android APIs), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` is deprecated. 

The real solution involves handling keyboard insets properly since .NET MAUI 10 now uses Android's edge-to-edge display model. You can either configure `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the manifest as a fallback, or better yet, use the new keyboard inset handling mechanisms to adjust your layout padding when the keyboard appears.
